info.title: Western Astrology API
info.description:
Production-ready Western astrology API + MCP for AI agents and developers. Natal birth charts, publisher-grade daily/weekly/monthly horoscopes with unique content per sign (house-based transit system, active transits, Moon sign, Moon phase, energy rating), synastry compatibility scores, composite charts, solar and lunar returns, real-time transit aspects, and moon phases. 4 house systems (Placidus, Koch, Whole Sign, Equal) with accurate tropical ephemeris. 23+ endpoints optimized for horoscope platforms, astrology dating apps, AI chatbots, wellness products, and zodiac content engines. Schedule future horoscopes with date parameter for editorial workflows. No astronomy expertise needed.

# RoxyAPI: AI-Native Insight Infrastructure

> **Base URL:** `https://roxyapi.com/api/v2`
> All endpoint paths below are relative to this base URL.

The only multi-domain spiritual intelligence API. 9 domains (astrology, tarot, numerology, crystals, dreams, I-Ching, angel numbers, Vedic astrology, location), 122+ endpoints, one API key, instant activation. Native MCP support for AI agents.

## Who uses RoxyAPI

- **Developers** building astrology apps, tarot platforms, numerology calculators, or dream journals
- **AI agent builders** connecting Claude, GPT, or Gemini to real calculation engines via MCP
- **Vibe coders** shipping insight apps with Cursor, Bolt, or Replit using zero domain knowledge
- **Founders and brands** launching branded spiritual experiences for their audience

## Quick start (60 seconds)

**1. Get your API key** at [roxyapi.com/pricing](https://roxyapi.com/pricing). Instant delivery, no account required.

**2. Make your first call:**
```bash
curl -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY" https://roxyapi.com/api/v2/tarot/draw -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"count": 3}'
```

**3. Monitor usage:**
```bash
curl -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY" https://roxyapi.com/api/v2/usage
```

## AI agent integration (MCP)

RoxyAPI has native Model Context Protocol support. Your AI agent auto-discovers all 122+ endpoints as callable tools with zero configuration:
- **Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf**: Add MCP server URL in settings
- **OpenAI Agents, Gemini ADK**: Connect via Streamable HTTP transport
- **Custom agents**: Use the MCP Python/TypeScript SDK

MCP endpoints: `https://roxyapi.com/mcp/{domain}` (e.g., `/mcp/astrology-api`, `/mcp/tarot-api`)

Setup guide: [roxyapi.com/docs/mcp](https://roxyapi.com/docs/mcp)

## Authentication

All endpoints require an API key via header or query param:
- **Header (recommended):** `X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY`
- **Query param (testing):** `?api_key=YOUR_KEY`

## Response format

Clean JSON, no wrapper objects. Errors return `{ "error": "message", "code": "error_code" }`. The `error` field is human-readable (may change wording). The `code` field is machine-readable (stable — safe to switch on programmatically).

Rate limit headers on every response: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Used`

## Errors

All errors return `{ "error": "message", "code": "error_code" }`:

| Status | Code | When |
|--------|------|------|
| 400 | `validation_error` | Missing or invalid parameters |
| 401 | `api_key_required` | No API key provided |
| 401 | `invalid_api_key` | Key format invalid or tampered |
| 401 | `subscription_not_found` | Key references non-existent subscription |
| 401 | `subscription_inactive` | Subscription cancelled, expired, or suspended |
| 404 | `not_found` | Resource not found |
| 429 | `rate_limit_exceeded` | Monthly quota reached |
| 500 | `internal_error` | Server error |

## Pricing

Flat per-request pricing. Every call counts the same, whether a planet position or a full birth chart with aspects. No credit systems, no variable costs. Plans from $39 to $699/month.

See [roxyapi.com/pricing](https://roxyapi.com/pricing)

## Resources

- [Quickstart guide](https://roxyapi.com/docs/quickstart) - first API call in 60 seconds
- [Documentation](https://roxyapi.com/docs) - guides, tutorials, domain reference
- [MCP setup](https://roxyapi.com/docs/mcp) - connect AI agents
- [Starter apps](https://roxyapi.com/starters) - clone and deploy in 30 minutes
- [FAQ](https://roxyapi.com/faq) - common questions
- [Contact](https://roxyapi.com/contact) - support and API key recovery

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. This is the exact calendar date when the person was born, used to calculate planetary positions for that specific day. Format must be YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 1990-07-15 for July 15, 1990).

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. This precise time determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Even small time differences (minutes) can change house placements. Use 12:00:00 if birth time is unknown (solar chart). Format: HH:MM:SS (e.g., 14:30:00 for 2:30 PM).

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive values are North, negative are South. This affects house cusps and local horizon calculations. Example: 40.7128 for New York City. Use geocoding APIs or location services to convert addresses to coordinates.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive values are East, negative are West. This affects house cusps and local time. Example: -74.006 for New York City. Use geocoding APIs to convert addresses to coordinates.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours (NOT minutes format). Negative for Western Hemisphere (west of UTC), positive for Eastern Hemisphere (east of UTC). Examples: New York EST = -5, Los Angeles PST = -8, London GMT = 0, Paris CET = 1, India IST = 5.5 (NOT 5:30), Tokyo JST = 9. IMPORTANT: Use decimal format for half-hour zones (India = 5.5, not 5:30). This converts LOCAL birth time to UTC for astronomical calculations (e.g., 14:30 with timezone 5.5 → 09:00 UTC). Calculations are timezone-independent - server timezone does NOT affect results.

houseSystem.description: House system for dividing the chart into 12 houses. Placidus (default) is most popular in Western astrology and time-sensitive. Whole Sign assigns one sign per house (simpler, ancient). Equal houses divide chart into 30° segments from Ascendant. Koch emphasizes houses in high latitudes. Choose based on astrological tradition or user preference.

post.summary: Get planetary positions - Ephemeris calculator for all planets
post.description: Calculate accurate tropical zodiac positions for all 10 planets (Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Pluto) for any date, time, and location. Returns longitude, latitude, zodiac sign, degree within sign, daily motion speed, and retrograde status. Perfect for transit tracking, ephemeris tables, astrology apps, and planetary position widgets. Verified against NASA JPL Horizons.

date.description: Target date for planetary positions in YYYY-MM-DD format. Use current date for transit positions, or any historical/future date for research. Planets move daily, so this date determines their zodiac positions.

time.description: Time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format for precise calculations. Moon moves ~13° per day, so time matters for accurate lunar position. Use 12:00:00 (noon) as default if exact time not needed.

latitude.description: Observer latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). While planetary longitudes are geocentric (same worldwide), this is needed for house calculations if extending functionality. For basic ephemeris, use 0 as default.

longitude.description: Observer longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Used for precise local time conversion. For basic planetary positions, this has minimal impact but ensures accuracy.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours (NOT minutes format). Negative for west of UTC, positive for east of UTC. Examples: New York EST = -5, India IST = 5.5 (NOT 5:30), Tokyo JST = 9, Nepal NPT = 5.75. IMPORTANT: Use decimal format (5.5, not 5:30).

post.description: Generate a lunar return chart for any month — cast for the exact moment the transiting Moon returns to its natal ecliptic longitude. A planetary return occurs when a transiting planet conjuncts its natal longitude, marking the beginning of a new cycle. Saturn return (~29 years) is the most significant life milestone in Western astrology. Jupiter return (~12 years) signals expansion and growth phases. Mars return (~2 years) resets energy and drive. longitude.description: Longitude of the return location in decimal degrees.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Output datetime is adjusted to this timezone.

houseSystem.description: House system for the return chart. Placidus (default), Whole Sign, Equal, or Koch.

birthDate.description: Original birth date used for natal planet longitude calculation.

planet.description: Planet whose return was calculated. Each planet has a different orbital period and return significance.

returnDate.description: Exact planetary return moment — when the transiting planet conjuncts its natal longitude. Adjusted to requested timezone. Marks the beginning of a new cycle for that planet in your life.

approximateCycle.description: Approximate orbital period for this planet. Mercury ~88 days, Venus ~225 days, Mars ~687 days (~1.9 years), Jupiter ~12 years, Saturn ~29 years.

latitude.description: Observer latitude used for house cusp calculation in the return chart.

longitude.description: Observer longitude used for Midheaven and local sidereal time.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC applied to output datetime formatting.

location.description: Location used for the planetary return chart house and Ascendant calculations.

date.description: Birth date in YYYY-MM-DD format. Determines planetary positions for the specific calendar day.

time.description: Birth time in 24-hour HH:MM:SS format. Determines the Ascendant (rising sign) and house cusps. Use 12:00:00 if unknown.

latitude.description: Birth location latitude in decimal degrees (-90 to 90). Positive = North, negative = South.

longitude.description: Birth location longitude in decimal degrees (-180 to 180). Positive = East, negative = West.

timezone.description: Timezone offset from UTC in decimal hours. Examples: New York = -5, London = 0, India = 5.5, Tokyo = 9.

birthDetails.description: Birth details used to generate this chart.

name.description: Planet name (Sun, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Pluto).

longitude.description: Tropical ecliptic longitude in degrees (0-360). Primary coordinate for zodiac sign and aspect calculations.

latitude.description: Ecliptic latitude in degrees. Near zero for most planets, varies for Moon and Pluto.

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign this planet occupies. Determined by 30-degree divisions of ecliptic longitude.

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign (0-29.999). Indicates how far the planet has progressed through the sign.

house.description: House placement (1-12). Determined by the selected house system and birth location.

speed.description: Daily motion in degrees per day. Negative values indicate retrograde motion.

isRetrograde.description: Whether the planet appears to move backward from Earth perspective. Retrograde periods signal review and introspection.

planets.description: All 10 planetary positions (Sun through Pluto) in the tropical zodiac with house placements.

number.description: House number (1-12). Each house governs specific life themes in Western astrology.

longitude.description: Ecliptic longitude of this house cusp in degrees (0-360).

sign.description: Zodiac sign on this house cusp. Colors the themes of this life area.

degree.description: Degree within the zodiac sign on this cusp (0-29.999).

houses.description: All 12 house cusps calculated using the selected house system.

houseSystem.description: House system used for this chart (placidus, whole-sign, equal, or koch).

planet1.description: First planet in the aspect pair.

planet2.description: Second planet in the aspect pair.

type.description: Aspect type. Major: conjunction (0), opposition (180), trine (120), square (90), sextile (60). Minor: semi-sextile, quincunx, semi-square, sesquiquadrate.

angle.description: Exact angular separation that defines this aspect type in degrees.

orb.description: Deviation from exact aspect in degrees. Tighter orb means stronger influence.

isApplying.description: Whether the aspect is applying (planets moving toward exact) or separating (moving apart). Applying aspects grow stronger.

strength.description: Aspect strength percentage (0-100). Based on orb tightness relative to the allowed maximum.

interpretation.description: Aspect nature. Harmonious (trine, sextile) flows easily. Challenging (square, opposition) creates tension and growth. Neutral (conjunction) blends energies.

aspects.description: All planetary aspects found in this chart with orbs, strength, and applying/separating status.

chart.description: Full tropical zodiac chart erected for the planetary return moment. Contains planetary positions, house cusps, aspects, Ascendant, and Midheaven.

longitude.description: Natal planet ecliptic longitude in degrees (0-360). The transiting planet returns to this exact degree.

sign.description: Tropical zodiac sign of the natal planet position.

natalPlanetPosition.description: Original natal planet position that defines the return. The transiting planet conjuncts this longitude to trigger the return.

summary.description: Narrative overview of this planetary return cycle and its significance for personal development.

keyThemes.description: Key life themes activated during this return cycle. Focus areas vary by planet — Jupiter brings expansion, Saturn brings structure and responsibility.

interpretation.description: Planetary return interpretation. Saturn returns (~29 years) mark major life milestones. Jupiter returns (~12 years) signal growth cycles. Inner planet returns offer shorter-term insights.

error.description: Human-readable error message. May change wording.
